verdict_reasoning: LYN (Lck/Yes-related novel tyrosine kinase) is a Src-family non-receptor tyrosine kinase anchored exclusively to the inner (cytoplasmic) leaflet of the plasma membrane via dual N-myristoylation and palmitoylation of its N-terminal SH4 domain. Its entire kinase domain, SH2, and SH3 domains face the cytoplasm; there is no transmembrane segment, no GPI anchor, and no extracellular domain. Pre-no checklist: (1) cell_state_induced — no documented outer-leaflet ecto-pool in cancer, apoptosis, stress, or immune activation; surface studies consistently show cytoplasmic-face localization; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — no narrow lineage with documented outer-leaflet display; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — LYN is not a lysosomal TM protein; (4) dual_localization — LYN does cycle between Golgi/endomembranes and the PM inner leaflet, but the PM pool remains inner-leaflet-anchored, not outer-leaflet-accessible; (5) stable_surface_attachment — LYN associates non-covalently with cytoplasmic tails of BCR/CD19/CD40 on the intracellular face, not the extracellular face. The protein body is not reachable from the extracellular space.
